Title: The impact of reducing fatty acid desaturation on the composition and thermal stability of rapeseed oil

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Microsomal omega-6 fatty acid desaturase; FAD2 family
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Cabriolet-derived EMS mutants K0472 or K0047
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maplus-derived HELP lines K0472-HE and K0047-HE
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Seed storage lipid biosynthesis; unsaturated fatty acid desaturation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid to linoleic acid polyunsaturated fatty acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Linoleic acid (C18:2), leading to linolenic acid (C18:3) formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond into oleic acid to form linoleic acid, thereby channeling flux from monounsaturated fatty acids toward PUFAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduced PUFA improves oil thermal stability; increased erucic acid enhances industrial feedstock value
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown versus glasshouse-grown seeds were analyzed for oil composition/stability; thermal stability tested at 120 °C by Rancimat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inactive mutant alleles reduce PUFA accumulation and increase oleic acid availability for elongation, indirectly increasing erucic acid content and strongly improving oil thermal stability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crossing/segregation with marker-assisted selection; Sanger sequencing of alleles; seed fatty acid profiling by GC; Rancimat thermal stability assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced PUFA content changes overall fatty acid balance; no explicit growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes Bna.FAD2.C5 as the particularly important FAD2 orthologue controlling PUFA proportion. Mutant alleles are denoted Bna.fad2.C5K0472 and Bna.fad2.C5K0047. Inactive Bna.fad2.C1 and Bna.fad2.A1 are discussed as background contributors but were not experimental focal genes in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mutant Bna.FAD2.C5 alleles were combined with inactive Bna.fad2.A5 in a high-erucic background to generate HELP lines. 2. Reduced conversion of C18:1 to PUFAs lowered C18:2+C18:3 from 24.9% in Maplus to 5.4–7.4% in HELP lines. 3. Reduced desaturation increased substrate availability for elongation, raising erucic acid to 53.7–54.4% and greatly improving thermal stability of oil.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable for breeding low-PUFA, high-stability rapeseed oils; can be stacked with active FAE1 alleles to create HELP oils for industrial applications and HOLP oils for food use.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Microsomal omega-6 fatty acid desaturase; FAD2 family
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Cabriolet-derived EMS mutants K0472 or K0047
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maplus-derived HELP lines K0472-HE and K0047-HE
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Seed storage lipid biosynthesis; unsaturated fatty acid desaturation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid to linoleic acid polyunsaturated fatty acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond into oleic acid to form linoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduced PUFA improves oil thermal stability; increased erucic acid enhances industrial feedstock value
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown versus glasshouse-grown seeds were analyzed for oil composition/stability; thermal stability tested at 120 °C by Rancimat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inactive allele contributes to low-PUFA phenotype, increasing oleic acid available for elongation to VLCFAs and supporting higher erucic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Marker-assisted selection; allele sequencing; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced PUFA content changes overall fatty acid balance; no explicit growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Required together with mutant Bna.FAD2.C5 for the low-PUFA trait in HELP development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Inactive Bna.fad2.A5 was selected with mutant Bna.fad2.C5 and active Bna.FAE1 alleles in HELP lines. 2. This allele combination strongly reduced PUFA content. 3. Reduced desaturation flux increased monounsaturated substrate availability for VLCFA elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of a low-PUFA allele stack for breeding thermally stable rapeseed oil, especially when combined with high-erucic loci.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAE1 condensing enzyme; very-long-chain fatty acid elongase KCS family
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maplus-derived HELP lines K0472-HE and K0047-HE
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid elongation branch producing eicosenoic and erucic acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Eicosenoic acid (C20:1) and erucic acid (C22:1) via elongation cycles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensing step of very-long-chain fatty acid elongation, extending oleic-derived acyl-CoAs toward eicosenoic and erucic acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial feedstock for oleochemicals such as erucamide; contributes to high-monounsaturate industrial oil profiles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown versus glasshouse-grown seeds were analyzed for composition and stability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active allele promotes elongation of C18:1 toward VLCFAs, enabling high erucic acid accumulation in HELP lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Marker-assisted selection; allele sequencing; seed fatty acid profiling by GC; genetic combination with low-PUFA alleles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Erucic acid increase is constrained by inability to incorporate VLCFAs at TAG sn-2; elongase activity may still be limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses that both elongation of C18:1 to C20:1 and C20:1 to C22:1 are mediated by the same elongase complex controlled by Bna.FAE1 loci. No allelic variation at FAE1 segregated within HELP lines.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Active Bna.FAE1.A8 was required with Bna.FAE1.C3 for high-erucic background in HELP lines. 2. Combined with reduced desaturation, this locus enabled erucic acid increase from 46.1% in Maplus to 53.7–54.4% in HELP lines. 3. Results support substrate supply and elongase capacity as determinants of erucic acid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential component of breeding high-erucic industrial rapeseed; should be stacked with low-PUFA fad2 alleles and potentially with improved LPAAT activity to further raise erucic acid.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAE1 condensing enzyme; very-long-chain fatty acid elongase KCS family
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maplus-derived HELP lines K0472-HE and K0047-HE
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid elongation branch producing eicosenoic and erucic acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Eicosenoic acid (C20:1) and erucic acid (C22:1) via elongation cycles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensing step of very-long-chain fatty acid elongation, extending oleic-derived acyl-CoAs toward eicosenoic and erucic acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial feedstock for oleochemicals such as erucamide; contributes to high-monounsaturate industrial oil profiles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown versus glasshouse-grown seeds were analyzed for composition and stability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active allele contributes to elongation-based accumulation of VLCFAs, especially erucic acid, in HELP lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Marker-assisted selection; allele sequencing; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Erucic acid accumulation remains limited by TAG positional constraints and possibly elongase capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed together with Bna.FAE1.A8 as the active orthologues conferring the high erucic trait.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Active Bna.FAE1.C3 was selected together with Bna.FAE1.A8 in HELP development. 2. In the low-PUFA background, active FAE1 loci supported increased total VLCFAs to 63.3%. 3. Variation in eicosenoic versus erucic acid among HELP plants suggests additional modifier loci beyond FAE1 genotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for high-erucic breeding and should be combined with low-PUFA alleles; further gains may require modifier loci or TAG engineering.
